commander_summary: Three of five actively monitored kill switches are in WARNING or beyond — most critically, the yield gap between posted (24.2%) and estimated organic (8.1%) yield exceeds its falsification threshold by nearly 20x, indicating a massive subsidy dependency whose funding source is unknown and classified as a suspicious absence. Surface indicators are unanimously healthy (par at $100.02, +$585M deposits, ASE +3.6%), creating a measurable divergence between structural deterioration and behavioral calm that defines a pre-loaded system. INCREASE_MONITORING: no kill switch is TRIGGERED and no compound stress leg is elevated, but the structural pre-load means the system's break point is lower than surface readings suggest, and the next observation period is essential to determine whether this is a deteriorating trend or a static-point snapshot. The central paradox: the architecture's visible success in maintaining par stability and attracting deposits is mechanically deepening the structural fragility that could eventually break it — and the single data point that would most change this assessment, the yield subsidy funding source, is the one most likely to be strategically withheld.
score_reasoning: Layer 2 scored bear pressure at 7 (on a 0-10 scale). Converting to the 0-100 scale: Layer 2's assessment maps to approximately 55-70. My final score of 62 reflects: (1) three kill switches in WARNING+ with the yield gap at extreme magnitude (upward pressure); (2) genuine surface stability across all compound stress legs (downward pressure); (3) the paradox resolution — structural pre-load is measured fact, but surface health is also measured fact; (4) burden of proof stripping three hidden move inferences that would have added speculative upward pressure; (5) null hypothesis acceptance on Consortium inaction reducing that specific threat by 2 points. The score reflects a system where the probability of thesis modification or falsification (85% combined per Layer 3) substantially exceeds the probability of confirmation (15%), but where no imminent catalyst is visible and surface indicators provide genuine near-term stability. Per LZ-EPH-002, confidence in the structural pre-load (measured) is higher than confidence in the timeline to any catalyst (predicted).
recommendation_reasoning: Three kill switches in WARNING+ with extreme yield gap magnitude demand escalated surveillance, but no kill switch is TRIGGERED and all surface compound stress indicators are firmly normal. EXIT_SIGNAL requires a TRIGGERED kill switch or CRITICAL compound stress, neither of which exists. REDUCE_EXPOSURE would be premature given genuine surface stability and the single-observation-period limitation per LZ-RC-002. INCREASE_MONITORING is the correct call because: the structural pre-load creates elevated catalyst sensitivity requiring faster detection cycles; the yield subsidy funding source is a SUSPICIOUS_ABSENCE that must be resolved; and the next observation period is critical for establishing whether the deterioration is a trend or a snapshot.

Behavioral evidence reinforces rather than mitigates the structural finding. The yield subsidy funding source is classified as SUSPICIOUS_ABSENCE, which adds opacity risk.

The feedback loop analysis (yield subsidy → deposit growth → concentration → subsidy burden amplification) is well-supported by data. However, Layer 3 correctly notes the actual break dynamics are theoretical. No change to score — the behavioral evidence neither strengthens nor weakens the structural observation. compounding_risks.outcome: A confidence shock simultaneously THREATENS capital (triggering deposit outflows, swap mechanism stress, and potential ASE dilution spiral in a pre-loaded structure) AND VALIDATES the analytical framework (confirming that the kill switch warnings correctly identified structural fragility masked by surface indicators), because the very conditions that make the system appear healthy (yield subsidy maintaining deposits, untested swap mechanism appearing robust, favorable narrative) are the same conditions that prevent structural verification and deepen the fragility they conceal.

Per LZ-EH-005, this is a genuine contradiction between structural indicators and behavioral indicators where both are verified from primary data. The structural data is measured directly. The behavioral data is measured directly. Neither is wrong. The resolution is that behavioral stability is CAUSED by information asymmetry (structural data is not visible to most participants) and by the yield subsidy suppressing the symptoms that would otherwise signal distress. Forcing a resolution by declaring one 'more true' would be arbitrary. The paradox itself — a structurally deteriorating system with behaviorally healthy surface — IS the finding. This paradox directly informs the compound stress assessment.

Both are simultaneously true and neither contradicts the other when properly understood. The deposits are real (data). The structural worsening is real (data). The contradiction is between what deposits SIGNAL (confidence) and what deposits CAUSE (fragility). Per LZ-EH-005, this contradiction is the finding. Positive inflows are genuine near-term stability support AND genuine structural fragility amplifiers simultaneously.

kill_switches.name: KS-1: Par Stability
kill_switches.detail: The measurement is unambiguous: $0.02 deviation is firmly GREEN. Layer 3's contextualization (lagging indicator, confidence-dependent) is valid analytical framing but does not change the measurement. Par IS stable. The qualification that stability is untested under stress is captured in KS-4.

kill_switches.name: KS-2: Facility Yield Sustainability
kill_switches.detail: This is the single most alarming finding in the entire assessment. The 198% gap against a 10% WARNING threshold is not a marginal exceedance — it is nearly 20x the threshold. Even under the most generous assumptions about organic yield underestimation (Layer 2's robustness test at 15% organic yield), the gap remains at 61%, still 6x the threshold. The WARNING status is technically correct per the thesis framework (there is no TRIGGERED level defined for KS-2), but the magnitude demands escalated monitoring. The yield subsidy funding source must be resolved as the highest priority intelligence requirement.

kill_switches.name: KS-3: Reserve Coverage Ratio
kill_switches.detail: Coverage is 2.8 percentage points above the 15% TRIGGERED threshold, providing some buffer. However, with +$585M net deposits this period and no visible collateral additions, the ratio is on a deteriorating trajectory. Each period of positive net deposits without corresponding collateral addition pushes the ratio toward TRIGGERED. The buffer is real but shrinking. Escalated monitoring is warranted to detect approach to 15%.

kill_switches.name: KS-4: Swap Mechanism Integrity
kill_switches.detail: By the thesis metrics, the swap mechanism is operating normally with zero ASE supply growth. The concern about untested behavior at scale is analytically valid but is not a kill switch finding — the mechanism has not been stressed, which means it has neither passed nor failed. Per LZ-RC-003, the absence of mechanism failure is not evidence of mechanism robustness, but it also cannot be scored as mechanism failure. GREEN with the analytical caveat that GREEN is measured, not battle-tested.

kill_switches.name: KS-5: Deposit Concentration
kill_switches.detail: 3.4 percentage point exceedance above WARNING threshold and actively worsening. The behavioral finding that deposits are yield-driven rather than conviction-driven amplifies the risk: high concentration of mercenary capital means coordinated exit risk. Escalated monitoring for any signal of depositor behavior change.

kill_switches.name: KS-6: Organic Demand
kill_switches.detail: Escalated from UNKNOWN to active MONITORING. The absence of organic demand data is strategically explained but the long-term thesis requires organic utility. If AFN has no settlement utility beyond the Facility, the entire architecture reduces to a yield product backed by subsidy — a fundamentally weaker proposition. Intelligence collection on AFN bilateral settlement activity is the second-highest priority requirement.

kill_switches.name: KS-7: Institutional Diversification
kill_switches.detail: Insufficient data to change status. The ASE institutional holder base is not visible in current data.

kill_switches.name: KS-8: Collateral Quality
kill_switches.detail: The collateral composition description is vague. 'Offshore sovereign bonds and commodity-linked instruments' spans a wide quality range. Without granular breakdown, quality assessment is impossible. This is a data gap but not actionable without more information.

kill_switches.name: KS-9: Competitive Architecture
kill_switches.detail: Pure data gap. The competitive environment affects depositor alternatives and therefore exit risk, but no data exists to assess it.

kill_switches.name: KS-10: Regulatory Posture
kill_switches.detail: No regulatory data to act on. The potential impact of adverse regulatory classification is high (could trigger compound stress across all three surface legs simultaneously), but absence of signals cannot be scored in either direction per LZ-RC-003.

burden_of_proof_applied.inference: Yield Gap Exceeds Falsification Threshold — Foundation Deliberate Subsidy Strategy
burden_of_proof_applied.reasoning: VALID classification with 1 assumption. Layer 2 provides two independent data points (posted yield 24.2% from primary market data, organic yield 8.1% from Layer 1 analysis) and the robustness test showing even at 15% organic yield the gap exceeds threshold at 61%. The inference that the Foundation is deliberately subsidizing is supported by actions (maintaining yield without adjustment, continuing deposit acceptance) per LZ-EH-003. Full weight.

burden_of_proof_applied.inference: Stabilization Consortium Inactivity — Par Defense Mandate Explanation
burden_of_proof_applied.reasoning: Null hypothesis accepted: Consortium's mandate is par defense, par is at $0.02 deviation, inaction is procedurally expected. Layer 2 confirms par stability and Consortium description as 'peg defense.' The null is sufficient per LZ-RC-004. This DOES NOT eliminate the coverage ratio concern — it explains Consortium behavior specifically. Full weight for the null.

burden_of_proof_applied.inference: Deposit Concentration — Mechanical Outcome of Yield Subsidy
burden_of_proof_applied.reasoning: Null hypothesis accepted: 24.2% yield creates dominant incentive to deposit in Facility rather than hold AFN elsewhere. Concentration is a predictable emergent outcome, not strategic manipulation. Layer 2 confirms both the yield and the concentration data. Null is sufficient and well-supported. The RISK of concentration remains regardless of its cause — the null explains origin, not impact.

burden_of_proof_applied.inference: Reserve Coverage Ratio — Structural Mismatch Between Growth and Backstop
burden_of_proof_applied.reasoning: VALID with 1 assumption (no hidden capital). Layer 2 provides primary data: $4.29B collateral, $24.05B outstanding, 17.8% ratio, inactive deployment, Day -12 last acquisition. The inference that deposit growth has outrun backstop capitalization is directly supported by the data. The null (scheduled replenishment) was correctly rejected — no evidence of any replenishment mechanism. Full weight.

burden_of_proof_applied.inference: Net Deposit Inflows — Rational Yield-Chasing with Incomplete Information
burden_of_proof_applied.reasoning: Null accepted: depositors see 24.2% yield and $100.02 par. Their behavior is straightforwardly rational. No strategic complexity needed. This null is itself a risk finding — yield-driven deposits are mercenary and will reverse if yield changes. Full weight for both the explanation and its risk implication.

burden_of_proof_applied.inference: Compound Stress Surface MONITORING vs Structural Pre-Load
burden_of_proof_applied.reasoning: VALID with 1 assumption (kill switch thresholds are meaningfully calibrated). Layer 2 independently verified three kill switches in WARNING+. The surface compound stress indicators are independently verified as normal. The divergence between surface and structural is a direct observation, not an inference. The assumption about threshold calibration is mitigated by the extreme magnitude of KS-2 (198% vs 10%). Full weight.

burden_of_proof_applied.inference: Favorable Narrative — Rational Assessment Based on Incomplete Information
burden_of_proof_applied.reasoning: Null accepted: analysts see the same healthy surface data depositors see. Their favorable assessments are rational given visible inputs. No active narrative manipulation needed as explanation. The risk implication — narrative fragility to information shocks — follows directly from the null. Full weight.

burden_of_proof_applied.inference: Atlas Foundation Seeking Additional Capital (Hidden Move)
burden_of_proof_applied.reasoning: FLAGGED (2 assumptions) × no data support = stripped. This is pure incentive-derived speculation. No observable evidence of fundraising activity. Layer 3 correctly flagged this at low confidence. Per burden of proof rules: FLAGGED at 50% weight, then no data support strips it entirely. This does not influence bear pressure score.

burden_of_proof_applied.inference: Facility Operators Modeling Yield Reduction Scenarios (Hidden Move)
burden_of_proof_applied.reasoning: FLAGGED (2 assumptions) × no data support = stripped. No evidence of contingency planning. Incentive logic is sound but produces no actionable signal. Logged for context only.

burden_of_proof_applied.inference: Institutional Depositors Preparing Exit Strategies (Hidden Move)
burden_of_proof_applied.reasoning: FLAGGED (2 assumptions) at 50% weight × partial data support (high ASE turnover as possible hedging) at 50% = 25% effective weight. However, the high ASE turnover is uninterpretable without benchmarks per Layer 2. The partial data support is itself uncertain. At 25% effective weight with uncertain supporting evidence, this is effectively stripped from scoring. Logged as context.

contradictions_resolved.data_says: Stabilization Consortium coverage at 17.8% below 20% WARNING — backstop is thinning.
contradictions_resolved.behavior_suggests: Layer 3 null hypothesis holds: Consortium's mandate is par defense, not ratio defense. Inaction is procedurally normal when par is at $0.02.
contradictions_resolved.reasoning: The null hypothesis explains the Consortium's BEHAVIOR but does not resolve the DATA concern. The coverage ratio is 17.8% regardless of why the Consortium is inactive. The backstop is objectively thinner than the WARNING threshold. Whether the Consortium is inactive by design or by constraint, the coverage gap is real. Data wins because the structural risk exists independently of the behavioral explanation. However, the behavioral context (par defense mandate) means the Consortium's inaction is not an additional negative signal on top of the coverage ratio — it is EXPLAINED by the mandate. Score impact: the coverage ratio threat retains its Layer 2 severity but does not get upward adjustment from Consortium behavioral analysis.

data_classifications.layer3_context: Layer 3 notes 'Multiple institutional partnerships announced but limited deployment evidence' from thesis context. No transaction data on AFN usage outside Facility.
data_classifications.reasoning: Genuine absence of data, not a suspicious absence. The architecture is 18 months old and the Facility offers 24.2% yield — the overwhelming incentive is to deposit in Facility, not use AFN in bilateral settlement. The absence of organic settlement evidence is CONSISTENT with the yield subsidy dynamics (why hold AFN at 0% when you can deposit at 24.2%?). This is a DATA_GAP that is strategically explained by the incentive structure but cannot be classified as TRUE_NEGATIVE without violating LZ-RC-003. The gap matters because organic demand is required for long-term thesis viability.

data_classifications.layer3_context: Layer 3 correctly found INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE. No regulatory signals of any kind in data feed.
data_classifications.reasoning: Pure data gap. An 18-month-old novel financial architecture with a debt-to-equity swap mechanism would reasonably attract regulatory attention, but absence of regulatory data in the feed could mean: (a) no regulatory action exists, (b) regulatory action exists but isn't captured in this data feed, or (c) regulatory review is underway but pre-decisional. Cannot distinguish between these possibilities. Classified as DATA_GAP rather than SUSPICIOUS_ABSENCE because there is no affirmative evidence of regulatory engagement — the absence is genuinely uninformative.

Pure data gap. The competitive environment is relevant to deposit stickiness and the Foundation's ability to maintain the yield subsidy without competitive pressure, but no data exists to assess it. Cannot classify as TRUE_NEGATIVE (no competition exists) or SUSPICIOUS_ABSENCE (competition is being hidden) without any evidence either way.

data_classifications.layer2_classification: DATA_AVAILABILITY_GAP — burn rate calculation flagged as speculative per LZ-EPH-003
data_classifications.layer3_context: Layer 3 identified this as the highest-priority intelligence requirement. The funding mechanism determines whether the subsidy depletes the backstop (Tier-1 collateral) or is funded externally.
data_classifications.reasoning: Upgraded from DATA_GAP to SUSPICIOUS_ABSENCE. The yield subsidy is the central economic reality of this system — a 16.1 percentage point gap requiring approximately $3B/year to fund. The ABSENCE of any visibility into how this is funded is suspicious because: (1) it is the single most material economic fact about the system, (2) the Foundation has incentive to obscure the funding source if it depletes reserves, and (3) per LZ-EH-003, the Foundation's actions (maintaining yield without disclosing funding mechanism) carry more weight than any implicit assurance of sustainability. This is the intelligence gap most likely to be strategically maintained rather than accidentally absent.

data_classifications.layer3_context: Layer 3 flagged this as third-priority intelligence requirement. Actual distribution critical for exit correlation modeling.
data_classifications.reasoning: Standard data gap. The estimated depositor count may or may not be accurate, and the distribution is unknown. This is normal information limitation rather than suspicious absence — deposit-level data is typically not publicly available for institutional facilities.

The gap between surface calm and structural tension is the widest possible configuration — everything that CAN look good DOES look good, while everything measured below the surface is deteriorating.

_layer4_raw.pressure_reasoning: Layer 2 scored bear pressure at 7 (on a 0-10 scale). Converting to the 0-100 scale: Layer 2's assessment maps to approximately 55-70. My final score of 62 reflects: (1) three kill switches in WARNING+ with the yield gap at extreme magnitude (upward pressure); (2) genuine surface stability across all compound stress legs (downward pressure); (3) the paradox resolution — structural pre-load is measured fact, but surface health is also measured fact; (4) burden of proof stripping three hidden move inferences that would have added speculative upward pressure; (5) null hypothesis acceptance on Consortium inaction reducing that specific threat by 2 points. The score reflects a system where the probability of thesis modification or falsification (85% combined per Layer 3) substantially exceeds the probability of confirmation (15%), but where no imminent catalyst is visible and surface indicators provide genuine near-term stability. Per LZ-EPH-002, confidence in the structural pre-load (measured) is higher than confidence in the timeline to any catalyst (predicted).
_layer4_raw.recommendation_reasoning: Three kill switches in WARNING+ with extreme yield gap magnitude demand escalated surveillance, but no kill switch is TRIGGERED and all surface compound stress indicators are firmly normal. EXIT_SIGNAL requires a TRIGGERED kill switch or CRITICAL compound stress, neither of which exists. REDUCE_EXPOSURE would be premature given genuine surface stability and the single-observation-period limitation per LZ-RC-002. INCREASE_MONITORING is the correct call because: the structural pre-load creates elevated catalyst sensitivity requiring faster detection cycles; the yield subsidy funding source is a SUSPICIOUS_ABSENCE that must be resolved; and the next observation period is critical for establishing whether the deterioration is a trend or a snapshot.
monitoring_triggers: ANY yield reduction by the Facility — even a small cut signals subsidy stress and could trigger deposit behavior change
First period of net outflows from the Facility — would break the confidence loop and begin testing deposit stickiness
Reserve coverage ratio approaching 15% TRIGGERED threshold — currently at 17.8% with 2.8pp buffer
ASE price decline >10% in a single period — would begin activating the reflexive dilution loop concern
Any regulatory statement, filing, or examination notice regarding AFN/ASE architecture
Concentration ratio exceeding 80% — mechanical worsening from continued deposits without AFN supply growth outside Facility
Consortium deployment becoming active — would signal either par defense need or proactive reserve management
Any observable change to Facility deposit terms (minimum hold periods, withdrawal notice, qualification requirements)
AFN deviation exceeding $0.50 — would move KS-1 from GREEN to elevated range
_layer4_raw.biggest_uncertainty: The yield subsidy funding source. Whether the ~$3B/year gap is funded from Tier-1 collateral drawdown (directly depleting the backstop, making KS-2 and KS-3 causally linked), Foundation operating capital (separate runway), or undisclosed revenue (potentially resolving the concern entirely) changes the entire risk architecture. This single unknown cascades into the reserve coverage trajectory, the compound stress pre-load assessment, and the thesis probability distribution. Per LZ-EPH-001, I cannot resolve this without the data.
_layer4_raw.what_would_change_assessment: To REDUCE_EXPOSURE: Any single monitoring trigger firing, OR a second consecutive observation period showing all three WARNING+ kill switches remaining beyond threshold (establishing trend per LZ-RC-002). To EXIT_SIGNAL: KS-3 reaching 15% TRIGGERED, OR first period of net outflows >$500M, OR compound stress reaching ELEVATED (two surface legs simultaneously elevated). To HOLD_POSITION (de-escalation): Confirmed evidence of the yield subsidy being funded from non-reserve capital with multi-year runway, OR observable organic demand development outside the Facility, OR reserve coverage increasing above 20% through new collateral acquisition.
_layer4_raw.final_report: Three of five actively monitored kill switches are in WARNING or beyond — most critically, the yield gap between posted (24.2%) and estimated organic (8.1%) yield exceeds its falsification threshold by nearly 20x, indicating a massive subsidy dependency whose funding source is unknown and classified as a suspicious absence. Surface indicators are unanimously healthy (par at $100.02, +$585M deposits, ASE +3.6%), creating a measurable divergence between structural deterioration and behavioral calm that defines a pre-loaded system. INCREASE_MONITORING: no kill switch is TRIGGERED and no compound stress leg is elevated, but the structural pre-load means the system's break point is lower than surface readings suggest, and the next observation period is essential to determine whether this is a deteriorating trend or a static-point snapshot. The central paradox: the architecture's visible success in maintaining par stability and attracting deposits is mechanically deepening the structural fragility that could eventually break it — and the single data point that would most change this assessment, the yield subsidy funding source, is the one most likely to be strategically withheld.

player_analysis.stated_position: Stable architecture with strong yield proposition. Multiple institutional partnerships announced. Confidence in the AFN par stability mechanism.
player_analysis.observed_actions: Posting 24.2% yield against an estimated 8.1% organic yield — a gap of 16.1 percentage points. Stabilization Consortium deployment is inactive. No visible collateral additions in at least 12 days. No yield adjustment despite deteriorating reserve coverage. No visible evidence of partnership deployments generating organic demand.
player_analysis.say_do_gap: LARGE. The Foundation's public narrative is stability and institutional adoption, but its RESOURCE COMMITMENT is overwhelmingly directed at sustaining an above-market yield that dwarfs organic economics. It is NOT committing visible resources to the things that would make the architecture self-sustaining: organic demand generation, reserve replenishment, or collateral expansion. Per LZ-EH-003, the resource allocation toward yield subsidy and the absence of resource allocation toward structural health are more meaningful than any partnership announcement.
player_analysis.inferred_strategy: The Foundation's revealed preference is growth-at-all-costs via yield subsidy, prioritizing deposit volume over structural sustainability. This is consistent with a strategy of reaching critical mass (network effects, liquidity depth, institutional entrenchment) before needing to prove organic economics. Whether this is a deliberate 'grow-then-monetize' strategy or an unsustainable Ponzi-like dynamic cannot be determined from current data — both produce identical observable behavior at this stage.

player_analysis.stated_position: Established as peg defense backstop with Tier-1 offshore collateral.
player_analysis.observed_actions: Deployment status: inactive. Last acquisition: Day -12. Coverage ratio has deteriorated to 17.8%, below the 20% WARNING threshold. No visible capital deployment, no visible collateral acquisition, no visible defensive action despite coverage ratio breach.
player_analysis.say_do_gap: SIGNIFICANT. The Consortium exists specifically to backstop par stability, yet it is taking no visible action while its own coverage ratio breaches the WARNING threshold. Per LZ-EH-003, inaction during a threshold breach is a stronger signal than any stated readiness. The Consortium is behaving as if either: (a) it does not consider current conditions threatening, (b) it is constrained from acting, or (c) it is preserving ammunition for a more severe scenario. All three interpretations carry different risk profiles but the inaction itself is the primary data point.
player_analysis.inferred_strategy: Most parsimoniously: the Consortium is either capital-constrained or operating under a mandate that triggers deployment only at more severe stress levels (e.g., actual par deviation, not just reserve ratio deterioration). The 12-day inactivity during a WARNING-level coverage breach suggests the coverage ratio itself may not be a deployment trigger in their framework — they may be anchored to par deviation (currently $0.02, well within normal). This interpretation is consistent with their mandate being par defense rather than ratio defense.

player_analysis.stated_position: Competitive yield proposition for institutional depositors.
player_analysis.observed_actions: Maintaining 24.2% posted yield without adjustment. Continuing to accept net inflows (+$585M). No visible yield reduction, no visible term changes, no visible depositor qualification changes.
player_analysis.say_do_gap: MODERATE. The gap is not between stated position and actions (they are delivering what they promise) but between actions and structural reality. They are maintaining an unsustainable yield level and continuing to accept deposits that worsen concentration. A rational operator aware of structural fragility would either reduce yield preemptively or restrict new deposits. They are doing neither, which suggests either (a) they do not perceive the structural risk, (b) they are under instruction from the Foundation to maintain yield, or (c) yield reduction would itself trigger outflows they cannot absorb.
player_analysis.inferred_strategy: The Facility is likely trapped in a yield maintenance imperative: reducing yield risks triggering the very outflows that would stress the system, while maintaining yield depletes the resources needed to withstand those outflows. This is a classic 'trapped position' dynamic. Per LZ-EH-003, the continued acceptance of +$585M in new deposits while concentration is already at 78.4% is the key action — it reveals that growth is being prioritized over structural risk management.

If depositors are primarily yield-motivated (as the ~$60.4M average position suggests institutional allocation), their behavior will reverse rapidly if yield is cut or par stability is questioned. feedback_loops.loop: Yield Subsidy → Deposit Growth → Concentration → Subsidy Burden Amplification
feedback_loops.trigger_to_next_state: Any event that forces yield reduction (reserve depletion, Foundation capital constraint, or competitive pressure forcing reallocation) would convert deposit growth into deposit flight, as yield-motivated depositors have no structural reason to stay. The transition would be from orderly growth to potentially disorderly outflows.
feedback_loops.thesis_implication: This loop directly challenges the thesis. Higher deposits increase the numerator of concentration (currently 78.4%), increase the absolute subsidy cost (16.1% gap applied to growing base), and dilute the coverage ratio (denominator grows while Tier-1 collateral is static). Each new deposit SIMULTANEOUSLY makes the system look healthier on surface metrics (confidence, growth) while making it structurally more fragile. This is a self-reinforcing fragility loop disguised as a growth loop.

feedback_loops.loop: Confidence → Par Stability → No Redemptions → No Swap Mechanism Testing → Assumed Mechanism Health → Confidence
feedback_loops.trigger_to_next_state: Any confidence shock (regulatory action, yield cut announcement, competing product at comparable yield, investigative reporting on subsidy dynamics) would break the confidence loop and generate the first meaningful test of the swap mechanism. The mechanism has only processed $78M against $24.05B outstanding — a stress test would be orders of magnitude larger.
feedback_loops.thesis_implication: This is a circular confidence loop where mechanism health is ASSUMED from the absence of stress rather than VERIFIED through stress. The thesis depends on swap mechanism integrity, but the mechanism's credibility currently rests on never having been seriously tested. This is a paradox: the conditions that confirm the thesis (stability) are the same conditions that prevent verification of the thesis (no stress test). Per LZ-RC-003, the absence of mechanism failure is not evidence of mechanism robustness.

feedback_loops.loop: Reserve Depletion → Consortium Inaction → Coverage Deterioration → Reduced Backstop Credibility → Potential Confidence Loss
feedback_loops.trigger_to_next_state: This loop activates when market participants become AWARE of the coverage ratio deterioration. Currently, the favorable narrative environment may be insulating sentiment from this data point. If coverage drops below 15% (TRIGGERED) or if the data becomes widely visible, the Consortium's credibility as backstop could be questioned, which removes a key pillar of the confidence loop above.
feedback_loops.thesis_implication: Challenges the thesis. The Consortium was established to provide backstop credibility, but its inaction during deteriorating coverage undermines that function. If the Consortium's credibility is questioned, the entire par stability mechanism loses its 'last resort' anchor.

feedback_loops.loop: ASE Price → Swap Mechanism Viability → AFN Redemption Cost → Redemption Incentive
feedback_loops.trigger_to_next_state: This loop is currently positive (rising ASE makes swaps favorable for the system). It becomes reflexively NEGATIVE if ASE declines: falling ASE → more ASE must be minted per AFN redeemed → ASE dilution → further ASE decline → more dilution required. This is a death spiral feedback loop that is theoretically possible but currently entirely dormant with ASE at +3.6%.
feedback_loops.thesis_implication: This is the thesis's central vulnerability and central validation simultaneously. If ASE remains strong, the swap mechanism works as designed. If ASE enters a sustained decline during a redemption event, the reflexive dilution spiral could overwhelm the mechanism. Currently dormant — but this is the specific loop that would convert orderly stress into disorderly cascade.

behavioral_patterns.pattern: Across all observable players, behavior is optimized for CURRENT conditions rather than STRUCTURAL resilience. The Foundation subsidizes yield rather than building organic demand. The Consortium hoards reserves rather than rebuilding coverage. The Facility accepts new deposits rather than managing concentration. Depositors chase yield rather than assessing structural risk. Every player is locally rational but collectively building fragility.

Information asymmetry between system operators and depositors. The Foundation and Facility operators presumably have visibility into the yield gap, reserve coverage trajectory, and concentration metrics. Depositors likely see the posted yield and par price. The behavioral divergence — operators continuing to accept deposits while structural indicators deteriorate — is consistent with either (a) operators believing they can manage the situation, or (b) operators extracting value while conditions allow. Both produce identical observable behavior.

Surface-indicator vs. structural-indicator divergence is the defining pattern. Every surface indicator (par stability, deposit growth, ASE appreciation, sentiment) reads positive. Every structural indicator measured (yield gap, reserve coverage, concentration) reads WARNING or beyond. This divergence is the single most important observation in the entire dataset.

strategic_inferences.finding_from_layer2: Yield Gap Exceeds Falsification Threshold (198% vs 10%)
strategic_inferences.null_hypothesis: The organic yield estimate of 8.1% is significantly wrong, and actual organic yield is much closer to 24.2%, meaning the gap is measurement error rather than a real subsidy.
strategic_inferences.rational_explanation: The Foundation is deliberately subsidizing yield to attract and retain institutional deposits during a growth phase, accepting capital depletion as the cost of building critical mass. This is a 'blitzscaling' strategy where user acquisition cost (yield subsidy) is tolerated because the Foundation believes network effects or future organic revenue will eventually close the gap.
strategic_inferences.alternative_explanation: The organic yield estimate methodology is flawed and the true organic yield is higher than 8.1%, but even so the gap is still materially above the 10% threshold — the Foundation is subsidizing, just less aggressively than the headline number suggests.
assumptions: The Foundation is choosing to subsidize rather than being forced to by a structural flaw in the yield generation mechanism
evidence_citations: Posted yield 24.2% from primary market data
Estimated organic yield 8.1% from Layer 1 analysis
Layer 2's robustness test showing even at 15% organic yield, gap still exceeds threshold at 61%
strategic_inferences.materialization_signal: Observable yield reduction by the Facility (indicating subsidy becoming unsustainable), OR observable increase in Tier-1 collateral (indicating subsidy being replenished), OR disclosure of additional revenue sources not captured in organic yield estimate.
strategic_inferences.reasoning: The null hypothesis fails because even under generous assumptions about organic yield underestimation, the gap remains far beyond the threshold. Layer 2's robustness test (even at 15% organic yield, 61% gap) makes measurement error an insufficient explanation for the magnitude observed. The Foundation's actions (maintaining 24.2% without adjustment, continuing deposit acceptance) are consistent with deliberate subsidy. The ONE assumption is whether this is strategic choice vs. structural necessity — but the existence of the subsidy is near-certain regardless.

strategic_inferences.finding_from_layer2: Stabilization Consortium Inactivity During Coverage WARNING Breach
strategic_inferences.null_hypothesis: The Consortium's deployment mandate is triggered by par deviation (currently $0.02), not by coverage ratio levels. Their inactivity is simply normal operating procedure because the par peg — their actual mandate — is intact.
strategic_inferences.rational_explanation: The Consortium is a par defense mechanism, not a ratio maintenance mechanism. Its inaction is expected when par deviation is $0.02. The coverage ratio WARNING is a thesis-defined threshold, not necessarily a Consortium operational threshold.
strategic_inferences.alternative_explanation: The Consortium is capital-constrained and unable to replenish reserves, and its inaction reflects inability rather than mandate adherence.
evidence_citations: Par deviation $0.02 well within $0.50 normal range
Consortium described as 'peg defense' in thesis context
Deployment status 'inactive' with last acquisition Day -12
strategic_inferences.materialization_signal: If null holds: Consortium deploys when par deviation approaches $0.50-$1.00. If alternative: Consortium fails to deploy even when par deviation exceeds WARNING levels.
strategic_inferences.reasoning: The simplest explanation is sufficient. The Consortium exists for par defense. Par is stable. Therefore inaction is expected. The coverage ratio is a thesis-defined monitoring metric, and there is no evidence the Consortium uses the same thresholds. This does NOT eliminate the concern about coverage deterioration — it simply explains the Consortium's specific behavior without requiring strategic inference. The coverage ratio problem remains real even if the Consortium's inaction is procedural.

strategic_inferences.null_hypothesis: This is the natural result of the Facility being the primary use case for AFN, and 78.4% concentration is simply what the architecture looks like during growth phase when organic demand has not yet developed.
strategic_inferences.rational_explanation: Concentration is a mechanical outcome of the yield subsidy attracting deposits faster than organic demand develops. It is not a strategic choice by any single player but an emergent property of the system's current economics.
strategic_inferences.alternative_explanation: The Foundation is deliberately concentrating AFN in the Facility to maintain control over the supply and prevent secondary market dynamics it cannot manage.
evidence_citations: $18.85B in Facility against $24.05B outstanding = 78.4%
24.2% posted yield creating dominant incentive to deposit in Facility vs. hold AFN elsewhere
strategic_inferences.materialization_signal: If concentration is structural rather than temporary: concentration ratio increases or remains above 75% even as AFN supply grows. If growth-phase artifact: concentration declines as organic use cases develop.
strategic_inferences.reasoning: When the Facility offers 24.2% yield, rational holders will deposit there rather than hold AFN elsewhere. The concentration is a predictable consequence of the yield premium, not evidence of strategic manipulation. However, the null hypothesis explaining WHY concentration exists does not eliminate the RISK that concentration creates. The finding that 78.4% concentration exceeds the WARNING threshold remains valid regardless of whether concentration is strategic or emergent.

strategic_inferences.finding_from_layer2: Reserve Coverage Ratio Below WARNING at 17.8%
strategic_inferences.null_hypothesis: Coverage ratio has declined mechanically as AFN outstanding supply grew, while the Consortium's collateral acquisition operates on a different (slower) cycle. The ratio is temporarily low but will be replenished through scheduled collateral additions.
strategic_inferences.rational_explanation: The Consortium's collateral ($4.29B) was calibrated for a smaller AFN supply and has not kept pace with growth ($24.05B outstanding). The Foundation's growth strategy (attracting deposits via yield subsidy) has outrun the Consortium's capitalization. This is a structural mismatch between growth rate and backstop capacity.
strategic_inferences.alternative_explanation: The Consortium's inactivity and static collateral represent a deliberate capital preservation strategy — deploying for ratio maintenance would deplete reserves needed for actual par defense.
assumptions: The Consortium's collateral base ($4.29B) has not been augmented in ways not visible in the data
evidence_citations: Coverage ratio 17.8% below 20% WARNING threshold from primary market data
Consortium established 6 months ago per thesis context while AFN supply has grown to $24.05B
+$585M net deposits this period further diluting coverage
strategic_inferences.materialization_signal: If structural mismatch: coverage ratio continues to decline absent new collateral additions. If temporary: observable Consortium collateral acquisition increasing Tier-1 value above $4.29B.
strategic_inferences.reasoning: The null hypothesis (scheduled replenishment) fails because there is no evidence of any replenishment mechanism or schedule. The Consortium was established 6 months ago with its current collateral, and the data shows no additions in at least 12 days during a period of continued deposit growth. The simplest sufficient explanation is that deposit growth has outrun the backstop's capitalization, creating a structural gap that widens with each new deposit. The ONE assumption (no hidden capital) is conservative given that Layer 2 flagged this same gap.

strategic_inferences.finding_from_layer2: Net Deposit Inflows of +$585M Despite Structural Deterioration
strategic_inferences.null_hypothesis: Depositors are making rational decisions based on the information available to them (24.2% yield, par at $100.02, positive sentiment), and the structural deterioration data is either not available to them or not material to their decision framework.
strategic_inferences.rational_explanation: Institutional depositors are yield-maximizing with a par-stability check. Both variables they are likely monitoring read positive. Their continued inflows are perfectly rational given their information set. There is no strategic mystery here — people deposit money where the yield is high and the asset looks safe.
strategic_inferences.alternative_explanation: Depositors are aware of structural concerns but believe the Foundation will manage them, effectively treating the yield premium as compensation for known risk.
evidence_citations: Net deposits +$585M from primary market data
Posted yield 24.2% well above market alternatives
AFN par at $100.02 providing clean risk signal to depositors
strategic_inferences.materialization_signal: If null holds: deposits reverse rapidly if yield is cut or par stability is questioned. If alternative (risk-aware depositors): deposits decline gradually even without a yield cut as structural data surfaces.
strategic_inferences.reasoning: The simplest explanation is sufficient and well-supported. 24.2% yield with apparent par safety is an obvious allocation decision for yield-seeking institutions. No strategic inference is needed to explain this behavior. However, the null hypothesis's validity is precisely what creates the vulnerability: if deposits are yield-driven rather than conviction-driven, they will exit with equal speed if the yield equation changes.

strategic_inferences.finding_from_layer2: Compound Stress Surface MONITORING vs Structural Pre-Load
strategic_inferences.null_hypothesis: The surface compound stress indicators (par deviation, net flows, ASE price) are the correct measures of system health, and the kill switch WARNING breaches represent monitoring thresholds that are conservative by design. The system is genuinely healthy.
strategic_inferences.rational_explanation: The compound stress matrix measures symptoms (par deviation, outflows, ASE decline). The kill switches measure causes (yield unsustainability, reserve inadequacy, concentration). The causes are deteriorating while symptoms remain suppressed. This divergence — deteriorating causes, dormant symptoms — is the characteristic pattern of a pre-loaded structure. The system is NOT currently in distress, but the conditions for distress to emerge from a smaller catalyst are being assembled.
strategic_inferences.alternative_explanation: The kill switch thresholds are miscalibrated and the WARNING breaches represent normal operating conditions for a system at this growth stage.
assumptions: The kill switch thresholds (10% yield gap, 20% coverage, 75% concentration) are meaningfully calibrated to actual risk boundaries
evidence_citations: KS-2 yield gap at 198% vs 10% threshold — even miscalibrated, this magnitude is extreme
KS-3 coverage at 17.8% vs 20% WARNING — regardless of exact threshold, collateral coverage of 17.8% against outstanding is objectively thin
All three compound stress surface indicators simultaneously reading normal (par $0.02, net inflows positive, ASE +3.6%)
strategic_inferences.materialization_signal: If pre-loaded: a moderate stress event (e.g., 5-10% ASE decline, first period of net outflows, any negative regulatory signal) produces disproportionate surface-indicator movement. If thresholds miscalibrated: the system absorbs moderate stress with minimal surface response.
strategic_inferences.reasoning: The null hypothesis fails primarily because of the yield gap magnitude. Even if every threshold were doubled, the 198% yield gap would still be in WARNING. The directional finding — that structural indicators are deteriorating while surface indicators remain healthy — is robust to threshold calibration debates. This IS the characteristic pattern of pre-loading: conditions that don't look dangerous precisely because the stress hasn't been tested yet. The primary explanation aligns with the thesis framework's own compound stress logic.

strategic_inferences.finding_from_layer2: Favorable Narrative Environment Diverging from Structural Indicators
strategic_inferences.null_hypothesis: The narrative is favorable because the publicly observable data IS favorable (par stability, yield delivery, ASE appreciation). Analysts are accurately reporting what they can see. There is no manipulation — just differential information access.
strategic_inferences.rational_explanation: Analysts and institutional researchers are evaluating the same surface metrics that depositors see. Their favorable assessments are rational given that information set. The narrative is not wrong about current conditions — it is incomplete about structural conditions.
strategic_inferences.alternative_explanation: The Foundation is actively managing narrative through selective information disclosure, investor relations, and favorable research sponsorship.
evidence_citations: Institutional confidence 'high' from market sentiment data
Multiple favorable institutional research notes published
AFN at $100.02 and ASE +3.6% providing genuinely positive surface data
strategic_inferences.materialization_signal: If null holds: narrative reverses rapidly when structural data becomes visible (a sudden shift, not gradual). If active management: narrative persists even after structural concerns are publicly raised.
strategic_inferences.reasoning: The favorable narrative is easily explained by the genuinely positive surface data. No strategic inference is required. However, the null hypothesis being correct is itself a risk factor: if the narrative is based on incomplete information, it is fragile to information shocks. A narrative built on selective perception reverses faster than one built on comprehensive assessment.

hidden_moves.likely_action: Seeking additional capital injection or credit lines to extend yield subsidy runway, potentially through private fundraising, strategic partnerships, or structured finance arrangements not visible in market data.
hidden_moves.incentive_basis: The yield subsidy is the Foundation's primary growth engine. If the subsidy is funded from visible Tier-1 collateral (~$4.29B), the runway is finite. Rational behavior is to secure additional funding before the runway is exhausted, and to do so privately to avoid signaling subsidy dependency.

The Foundation's 18-month history with VC backing suggests fundraising capability. The continued +$585M inflows suggest the Foundation still sees value in growing the deposit base, which only makes sense if they believe they can sustain the subsidy.
hidden_moves.materialization_signal: Announcement of new strategic investor, credit facility, or treasury replenishment. Alternatively, sudden increase in Tier-1 collateral value without visible market acquisition.

Rational risk management requires contingency planning for yield adjustment. The question is whether they are modeling soft landings (gradual reduction) or preparing for emergency cuts.

thesis_confirmed.key_evidence: Par stability currently holds ($0.02 deviation). Swap mechanism operating normally. ASE appreciating. Net deposits positive. All surface indicators healthy. For full confirmation, the architecture would need to demonstrate organic yield growth closing the 16.1pp gap, reserve replenishment above 25%, and deposit concentration declining below 75% — none of which show positive trajectory. The 15% reflects the possibility that the Foundation has non-visible resources or revenue sources that resolve the structural gaps.
thesis_confirmed.timeline: 6-12 months for confirmation. Would require sustained organic yield growth and reserve replenishment visible in data.

thesis_modified.modification: The architecture may function as designed but requires ongoing Foundation subsidy that has not been disclosed as a structural dependency. The thesis would be modified from 'self-sustaining architecture' to 'subsidized architecture with credible backstop, viable if subsidy runway is sufficient and organic demand eventually materializes.' This is materially different from the original thesis but does not necessarily result in par failure.
thesis_modified.key_evidence: The massive yield gap (198%) is the primary evidence that the architecture is not self-sustaining. However, par stability holds and the swap mechanism works, suggesting the architecture's MECHANISM is sound even if its ECONOMICS are subsidized. The modification recognizes that mechanism integrity and economic sustainability are separate questions.

thesis_falsified.key_evidence: Kill Switch #2 is massively beyond WARNING (198% vs 10% threshold). Kill Switches #3 and #5 are in WARNING. The compound pre-load pattern is present. The subsidy dependency creates a finite runway regardless of mechanism integrity. Falsification would be confirmed by: yield reduction triggering deposit outflows → concentration unwind → swap mechanism tested at scale → reflexive ASE dilution → par deviation beyond $3.00 TRIGGERED level.
thesis_falsified.confirming_signal: First observable yield reduction by the Facility, OR first period of net outflows exceeding $500M, OR reserve coverage dropping below 15% TRIGGERED level, OR ASE price declining >15% in a single period triggering compound stress. Any of these would significantly increase falsification probability. Multiple occurring simultaneously would be near-definitive.

compound_stress_inference.orderly_vs_disorderly: Currently orderly at the surface level — all three compound stress legs (par deviation, net flows, ASE price) read normal. However, 'orderly' describes the SYMPTOMS, not the STRUCTURE. The structure is pre-loaded with three kill switch WARNING+ breaches creating conditions where the transition from orderly to disorderly requires less external force. The system is in what might be termed 'calm pre-stress' — outwardly stable, structurally loaded.
compound_stress_inference.catalyst_proximity: No single catalyst is imminent in the observable data. However, the system's vulnerability surface is wide: (1) any yield reduction announcement could trigger deposit exits; (2) any negative regulatory signal could break the confidence loop; (3) any ASE price shock >15% could activate the reflexive dilution loop; (4) any competing product at comparable yield could offer depositors an exit path; (5) any investigative reporting on the yield subsidy dynamics could shift narrative. The proximity is not defined by a single identifiable threat but by the BREADTH of potential triggers combined with the REDUCED threshold needed to activate compound stress. Per LZ-EPH-001: I don't know the exact catalyst proximity, but the pre-loaded structure means it is closer than surface indicators suggest.
compound_stress_inference.pre_load_assessment: Three legs are pre-loaded: (1) KS-2 yield gap at 198% — massively beyond WARNING — indicating the subsidy structure is under stress even if not visibly failing; (2) KS-3 reserve coverage at 17.8% — below WARNING, trending worse — indicating the backstop is thinning; (3) KS-5 deposit concentration at 78.4% — above WARNING — indicating exit correlation risk is elevated. These three pre-loads interact: the yield subsidy depletes resources (worsening coverage), deposit growth increases subsidy cost (worsening the yield gap), and concentration means any deposit exit is systemically significant (amplifying any stress event). The pre-load is not just additive — it is potentially multiplicative through these feedback connections.
compound_stress_inference.thesis_paradox: The thesis presents a fundamental paradox that must be stated honestly: the violent unwind scenario is simultaneously the HIGHEST RISK to capital and the HIGHEST VALIDATION of the analytical framework. If the pre-loaded structure breaks — triggering deposit outflows, swap mechanism stress, and ASE dilution spiral — it would confirm that the kill switch framework correctly identified structural fragility that surface indicators masked. But if the structure continues to hold — the Foundation successfully extends the subsidy, organic demand develops, and reserves are replenished — the kill switch warnings will appear to have been false alarms, and the structural pre-load diagnosis will look like overreaction to static-point threshold breaches. This analysis cannot resolve the paradox because the same pre-loaded conditions are consistent with both 'system that is about to break' and 'system that is growing through an awkward adolescent phase.' The distinction depends on the Foundation's actual capital position, organic demand trajectory, and external catalyst probability — all of which are intelligence gaps. Per LZ-EPH-001, the honest output is: the structure IS pre-loaded (this is measured, not predicted), the break point IS lower than an unloaded structure (this follows logically), but WHETHER a catalyst arrives before the structure matures is genuinely unknown.

_layer3_raw.inference_summary: Player behavior reveals a system where every participant is locally rational but collectively assembling fragility. The Foundation subsidizes growth, the Consortium preserves capital, the Facility accepts deposits, and depositors chase yield — all individually logical, all structurally compounding. The critical insight that data alone does not reveal is the INFORMATION ASYMMETRY: surface indicators visible to most participants read healthy while structural indicators read WARNING across three dimensions. This divergence means the system's stability depends on structural data NOT becoming widely visible — making the system's health contingent on information suppression rather than structural soundness. Layer 4 should focus on: (1) resolving the yield subsidy funding source as the single highest-value intelligence gap, (2) assessing whether the Foundation has non-visible capital that changes the runway calculation, and (3) determining whether the current pre-load state is a temporary growth-phase artifact or a deteriorating structural condition — which requires at minimum two more observation periods to distinguish a snapshot from a trend per LZ-RC-002.

compound_stress.pre_loaded_detail: NOTE: The thesis Compound Stress Matrix uses AFN deviation, Facility net outflows, and ASE price decline as its three legs — NOT USD/JPY, JGB 10Y, and Brent. Applying the ACTUAL thesis framework: Leg 1 (AFN deviation $0.02) = normal. Leg 2 (Facility net deposits +$585M, not outflows) = normal. Leg 3 (ASE +3.6%, not declining) = normal. None of the three compound stress legs are currently elevated per the thesis-defined thresholds. HOWEVER, the structural pre-loading from Kill Switches #2, #3, and #5 all being in WARNING or beyond means the structure is loaded in a dimension NOT captured by the Compound Stress Matrix's surface indicators. The Compound Stress Matrix measures SYMPTOMS (par deviation, outflows, ASE crash); the kill switches measure CAUSES (yield unsustainability, reserve inadequacy, concentration). The causes are deteriorating while the symptoms remain dormant. This is the core analytical finding: the compound stress matrix reads 'MONITORING' while the subsurface structural indicators read 'pre-loaded.' The matrix is technically at MONITORING because no surface legs are elevated, but the structure's break point is lower than an unloaded structure would have.
compound_stress.stress_chain_proximity: Surface indicators suggest comfortable distance from violent unwind. Subsurface indicators (3 kill switches in WARNING+) suggest the distance is significantly shorter than surface readings imply. A catalyst event (confidence shock, competing product launch, regulatory action) would need to traverse less distance to trigger compound stress than the surface matrix suggests. Precise proximity is unresolvable with current data — per LZ-EPH-001, declaring this 'I don't know the exact proximity but it's closer than the surface suggests' is the accurate output.

_layer2_raw.bear_pressure_reasoning: Three of five actively monitored kill switches are in WARNING or beyond: KS-2 (yield gap, massively exceeded), KS-3 (reserve coverage, below WARNING), KS-5 (concentration, above WARNING). This is the dominant signal. COUNTERBALANCING: KS-1 (par stability) is firmly normal, KS-4 (swap mechanism) shows zero stress, net deposits are positive, ASE is appreciating, and narrative is favorable. The surface appears healthy. RESOLUTION: The bear pressure score of 7 reflects the structural pre-loading that three kill switch exceedances represent, discounted by the genuine surface stability. It is NOT a prediction of imminent failure — per LZ-RC-005, the distinction between measurement (three kill switches breached) and prediction (this will lead to collapse) must be maintained. The previous score was 0, which in retrospect appears to have either predated these threshold breaches or to have been weighted entirely on surface indicators. The jump from 0 to 7 represents the recognition that structural health metrics have materially deteriorated while surface stability masks this deterioration. Per LZ-RC-002, this is the first period I am assessing, so I cannot determine if these threshold breaches represent a trend vs. a single snapshot — but the absolute levels are concerning regardless of trajectory.
_layer2_raw.layer2_summary: Three of five actively monitored kill switches are in WARNING or beyond (yield gap at 198% vs 10% threshold, reserve coverage at 17.8% below 20% threshold, deposit concentration at 78.4% above 75% threshold), while surface indicators remain healthy (par at $100.02, positive net deposits, ASE appreciating). This creates a structural pre-load condition where the system appears stable but its break point is reduced. Layer 3 should investigate: (1) the actual funding source for the yield subsidy — the ~$3B/year estimate is speculative per LZ-EPH-003 but the existence of a significant subsidy is near-certain given the yield gap magnitude; (2) why the Stabilization Consortium is inactive while coverage deteriorates; (3) whether any organic AFN demand exists outside the Facility. Three threats remain unscored due to complete data absence (organic demand, regulatory status, competitive landscape) — these are intelligence gaps, not negative findings. The compound stress matrix reads MONITORING at the surface but the structural pre-load from three breached kill switches means less catalyst force is needed to trigger surface stress indicators.
